hypothesis: If FREED++ optimizes a dual-objective reward that maximizes pharmacophoric similarity to a reference set of 53 estrogen receptor antagonists/degraders and minimizes structural similarity to known compounds, then the generated molecules will achieve high pharmacophore alignment with ER antagonists/degraders while remaining novel relative to ChEMBL, ZINC, and PubChem, and will exhibit favorable docking scores, QED, and synthetic accessibility.
